name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 3049 W Capitol Ave, West Sacramento, CA 95691 is open 8:30 AM – 8:30 PM daily. Call +1 916-372-7080.
description_long: Western Union is an established financial services company that has provided money transfer and payment solutions for decades. The company operates through an extensive network of agent locations including retail partnerships with grocery stores (City Market, Safeway, Kroger), convenience stores (Circle K), and major retailers (Walmart). Their primary business model relies on physical agent locations where customers can initiate and complete transactions. Western Union offers multiple money transfer options including online-initiated transfers with in-store payment, cash pickup services, bill payment capabilities, money order services, and mobile top-up functionality. The company distinguishes itself through its global reach, extended operating hours at many locations (some open until 11:59 PM), 24/7 online transaction initiation, and integration with major retail partners for accessibility. Additionally, they provide specialized services like inmate payment processing and prepaid card options through Western Union Prepaid. However, prospective users should note that the company's service availability depends entirely on agent location hours, transaction fees are not transparently displayed on the location-finder page, and the service model requires customers to visit physical locations for cash transactions—making it less convenient than purely digital alternatives for time-sensitive needs.
address: 3049 W Capitol Ave, West Sacramento, CA 95691

company_info.headquarters: 3049 W Capitol Ave, West Sacramento, CA 95691

pros: Extensive network of 150+ locations across the US with major retail partnerships (Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Circle K)
24/7 online transaction initiation at WU.com with option to pay in-store during agent hours
Extended hours at many locations, with some agents open until 11:59 PM for cash pickup
Multiple service options including wire transfers, money orders, bill payment, and mobile top-up
Specialized services like inmate payment and Western Union Prepaid card offerings
Real-time transfer tracking capability available online
Multi-language support including Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese
cons: Service availability entirely dependent on individual agent location hours—many locations close at 8:00 AM or 10:00 PM, limiting access
No transparent fee information displayed on website; customers must visit location or contact to learn transfer costs
Requires physical location visit for cash pickup—not suitable for customers needing purely digital/instant solutions
Technical issues noted on website (Orange wallet temporarily unavailable), indicating potential platform reliability concerns
Limited service options at some locations (e.g., Circle K location restricted to money orders only)
best_for: Customers sending money to family members internationally who prefer cash pickup over bank deposits
People without bank accounts seeking bill payment and money order services
Consumers in rural or underserved areas with limited banking infrastructure but access to retail agent locations
Individuals needing inmate payment services or specialized money transfer options
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked and underbanked consumers who need money transfer and payment services through convenient retail locations, or individuals sending international remittances. The main caveat is that service quality and availability depend heavily on individual agent location hours, and the lack of transparent pricing on their website makes it difficult to compare costs before visiting a location.
services: Online money transfer initiation (24/7 at WU.com)
In-store cash pickup at agent locations
